zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Heath於1525年出生在英國時，他的父親Robert Heath20歲，母親N.N. Heath60歲。他於1549年與Alice在英國英格蘭賀特福爾格亞特 阿維爾結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1593年在英國賀特福爾韋爾享年68歲，埋葬在英國賀特福爾韋爾。

nameMeanings.name: Heath
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived ‘(on the) heath’ (Middle English he(a)th, Old English hǣth) or else a habitational name from any of numerous places called with this word. The same word also denoted heather, the characteristic plant of heathland areas. This surname has also been established in Dublin, Ireland, since the late 16th century.

variants.name: Haith
meanings: English (Lincolnshire and East Yorkshire): variant of   Heath  .

variants.name: Heathman
meanings: English: topographic name denoting a dweller ‘on the heath’, from Middle English he(a)th + man(ne). Compare Heath and Heather .

variants.name: Hatton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places called Hatton (Cheshire, Derbyshire, Lincolnshire, Middlesex, Shropshire, Staffordshire, Warwickshire), Hatton Hall (Cheshire), or Cold and High Hatton (Shropshire). The placenames derive from Old English hǣth ‘heath, heather’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Heath .
English: variant of Atton with prosthetic H-, a topographic name from Middle English atte toun ‘(dweller) at the settlement’ (Old English æt thǣm tūn(e)). Compare Towne .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Chatáin, a patronymic from a personal name meaning ‘servant of (Saint) Catan’; see McHatton .

variants.name: Heather
meanings: English: topographic name denoting a ‘dweller on the heath’, an agent derivative of Middle English hethe; compare Heath . The name may also be habitational name from Heather (Leicestershire), from Old English hǣthor ‘heathland’ (hǣth ‘heath, heather’ + the noun suffix -or) or hǣddre ‘heather’. This surname is widespread in southern England, and also established in Ireland.

variants.name: Health
meanings: Altered form of English and Scottish   Heath  .
English and Scottish: perhaps a nickname from the vocabulary word health. — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is 105.

variants.name: Hoad
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, from Middle English hoth ‘heath’ (Old English hāth, a byform of Old English hǣth), or a habitational name from a place so named, such as Hoath in Kent. This form was restricted in the Middle Ages to southeastern England, and the surname is still largely confined to Kent, Surrey, and Sussex. Compare Heath .

variants.name: Heid
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter, Eckhart, Gerd, Gotthilf, Heinz, Klaus, Konrad, Kurt, Manfred, Ralf, Uli.
German, Swiss German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name from Middle High German heide, German Heide ‘heath, moor, open, uncultivated land’. Compare Heath .
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Haido, a short form of any of various compound names beginning with haid ‘heath’ (i.e. wasteland, often with heather).

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eideard
meanings: Gaelic equivalent of Edward .

variants.name: Édouard
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Edvard

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Heath was born in 1525, in England, his father, Robert Heath, was 20 and his mother, N.N. Heath, was 60. He married Alice in 1549, in Great Amwell, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 5 daughters. He died in 1593, in Ware, Hertfordshire, England, at the age of 68, and was buried in Ware, Hertfordshire, England.

Il est décédé en 1593, à Ware, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterré à Ware, Hertfordshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Edward Heathが1525年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Robert Heathは20歳，母N.N. Heathは60歳であった。1549年にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアグレアト アムウエルでAliceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1593年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアワーで68歳で死去し，イギリスハートフォードシュアイアワーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Heath(이)가 1525년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Heath(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 N.N. Heath(은)는 60세였다. 그는 1549년 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 그리트 아웰에서 Alice(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1593년 영국 허트퍼드실르 워에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허트퍼드실르 워에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Alice em 1549, em Great Amwell, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 5 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Heath родился в 1525 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени Robert Heath было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали N.N. Heath, 60. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice в 1549 г. в местности грит амвелл, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер в 1593 г. в местности вар, хиртфордшир, Англия в возрасте 68 лет и был похоронен в местности вар, хиртфордшир, Англия.
